topic.name: Financial Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and oversight in financial reporting and governance within federal agencies.

Document 1: Promoting Transparency in Financial Reporting Act of 2007
requires the SEC, the Financial Accounting Standards Board, and the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board to provide oral testimony
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to complete an audit of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
followed by a detailed report to Congress
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968
creates a new term of service for the office of Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the expansion and modification of Medicare and Medicaid services and fraud prevention measures.

Document 1: expands the authority of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to exclude from participation in federal health programs
establishes criminal penalties for the illegal purchase, sale, or distribution of federal health program beneficiary or provider numbers
Document 2: allows medical assistance under the Medicaid program to include payment for inpatient, nonhospital, residential substance use disorder treatment
amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Document 3: alters coverage requirements under a state plan for medical assistance
shall also provide biennial medical assistance to the individual for one cranial prosthesis

topic.name: Education Policy and Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at shaping funding and access to educational programs and services in both higher education and community schools.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid
makes eligible for Direct Loan program and Federal Perkins Loan program participation an educational program that provides at least 250 hours of instruction
Document 2: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit waiver authority of the Department of Education
prohibits ED from waiving statutory provisions that ban Pell Grant eligibility and federal financial aid for incarcerated students
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants
requires such schools to participate in community-based efforts to coordinate educational, developmental, family, health, and other comprehensive services

topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: Legislation focused on the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical wrappers of textile materials.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on filament tow of rayon.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Thiencarbazone-methyl, Isoxadifen-ethyl, and Tembotrione.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on healthcare provisions and support for veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits recovery of costs for non-service-connected disabilities from third parties for service-connected care.
Authorizes care and services provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 2: Authorizes the city of Aurora to convey land for construction of a veterans' medical facility.
Document 3: Establishes an Office of the Ombudsman in the Veterans Health Administration.
Serves as a last resort for veterans' health care and benefits complaints.

topic.name: Regulation of Energy Resources
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating fees, emissions, and safety standards related to energy production and environmental protection in the oil and gas industry.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases
on outer Continental Shelf land in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, Regulation and Enforcement employee to be subject to random testing
Prohibits an individual from being employed by any person conducting any activity under a lease or permit issued by BOEMRE.

topic.name: Border Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing border security through the construction of physical barriers and related measures.

Document 1: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to provide that fencing that does not effectively restrain pedestrian traffic may not be used
require the Secretary to complete construction of such fencing and installation of related equipment by December 31, 2010
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to provide that fencing that does not effectively restrain pedestrian traffic may not be used
require the Secretary to complete construction of such fencing and installation of related equipment by December 31, 2010

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight and accountability of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons
Document 2: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons

topic.name: Land and Wilderness Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the transfer and designation of land and wilderness areas in the United States.

Document 1: transfers, upon the request of the city of Flagstaff, Arizona, and the Burlington Northern and Santa Fe Railway Company, to a specified parcel of land
any reversionary interest of the United States in and to a specified parcel of land conveyed to the city.
Document 2: amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988
redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness.'
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey the Forest Service Lake Hill Administrative Site
in Summit County, Colorado.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving public safety and welfare concerning vulnerable populations, particularly unaccompanied alien children and sex offenders.

Document 1: Finding Fugitive Sex Offenders Act of 2011
authorize the United States Marshals Service to issue administrative subpoenas solely for the investigation of unregistered sex offenders.
Document 2: Our Communities, Our Choices Act of 2015
require consultation with state and local elected officials before awarding grants or contracts for non-federal housing facilities for unaccompanied alien children.
Document 3: UAC State Notification Act of 2014
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify the governor of a state within 48 hours before the placement of an unaccompanied alien child.

topic.name: Youth Mental Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on improving mental health research and intervention services for young people.

Document 1: establish a Youth Mental Health Research Network
focus primarily on the early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people
Document 2: autism education, early detection, and intervention program
developmental disabilities surveillance and research program
Document 3: provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals with mental illness
comprehensive community-based mental health systems

topic.name: Controlled Substances Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, monitoring, and approval processes for controlled substances and pharmaceuticals in the United States.

Document 1: requires a state that receives a prescription drug monitoring program implementation and enhancement grant to set aside at least 20% of grant funds to facilitate interstate electronic information sharing about prescriptions for controlled substances.
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that provisions of such Act related to marihuana shall not apply to any person acting in compliance with state laws relating to the production, possession, distribution, dispensation, administration, or delivery of marijuana.
Document 3: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration to facilitate the development and expedite the review of a new drug, biological product, or device that has been approved in the European Union.

topic.name: Water Rights and Reclamation Projects
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to water rights, reclamation projects, and water recycling initiatives in cooperation with local entities in Arizona and California.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with Pima County, Arizona, to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities
enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 2: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the city of San Juan Capistrano, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility
to expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities

topic.name: Housing and Economic Assistance
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing financial relief and support for homeowners and the housing market.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to provide assistance in specified amounts to the National Foreclosure Mitigation Counseling Program
foreclosure mitigation counseling activities in connection with the Home Affordable Modification Program
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase by 25% the allowable tax deduction for mortgage interest
waive the requirement to repay credit amounts under the first-time homebuyer tax credit
Document 3: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act to revise the limitation on the Secretary of the Treasury's authority to purchase troubled assets
Prohibits the Secretary from exercising any authority to make any such purchases with regard to any TARP amount authorized

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation, protection, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities
Document 2: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress
Document 3: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Nutrition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural practices, food assistance programs, and child nutrition initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to end agricultural direct payments
related to agricultural practices
Document 2: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act
direct certification for children eligible for free meals under school programs
Document 3: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act
reduce crop insurance assistance for crops grown on native sod acreage

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the promotion and regulation of renewable energy sources and technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program.
Requires renewable fuel to be advanced biofuel.
Document 2: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 to allow states to set energy rates.
Supports the sale of electric energy from renewable sources.
Document 3: Amends the National Energy Conservation Policy Act to support alternative fueled vehicles.
Authorizes federal agencies to participate in renewable energy programs.

topic.name: Health Information Security and Access
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, disclosure, and access of health-related information and reports.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify individuals of breaches of personally identifiable information.
Requires annual reporting to Congress on breaches and security measures.
Document 2: Requires the Government Publishing Office to provide public access to congressionally mandated reports.
Agencies must redact information not publicly released under the Freedom of Information Act.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for federal officers to willfully disclose protected health information.
Liability for unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable information.

topic.name: Accountability in Tax and Public Service
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and prosecutorial measures related to tax preparation, identity theft, and misconduct by public officials.

Document 1: Justice for Victims of IRS Scams and Identity Theft Act of 2016
expedite the review of identity theft cases
Document 2: Public Official Accountability Act
two-year increase in the prison sentence of public officials convicted of crimes
Document 3: Tax Return Preparer Accountability Act of 2015
regulate tax return preparers who are not otherwise subject to regulation

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Oversight
topic.description: Legislation regarding the regulation and oversight of financial institutions and their interactions with federal agencies.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency' that may share information with another covered agency or any other federal agency without waiving any privilege applicable to the information.

Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving, destroying, or affecting any privilege that may be claimed with respect to such information under federal or state law.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency' that may share information with another covered agency or any other federal agency without waiving any privilege applicable to the information.

Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving, destroying, or affecting any privilege that may be claimed with respect to such information under federal or state law.
Document 3: Prohibits Members of Congress, senior congressional staffers, and administration executives from purchasing or selling registered securities, futures, swaps, security futures products, security-based swaps, and options on them.
Subjects any person who violates such requirement to specified federal administrative penalties relating to material nonpublic information received in the course of employment.

topic.name: Wildlife Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the protection and management of wildlife, particularly migratory birds and the prohibition of animal fighting.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to permit the taking of any migratory game bird.
Contains provisions related to agricultural practices affecting migratory birds.
Document 2: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to allow the Department of the Interior to set costs for hunting stamps.
Limits the cost of the stamp and permits waivers for certain individuals.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful to sponsor or exhibit an animal in an animal fighting venture.
Imposes fines and prison terms for violations related to animal fighting.

topic.name: Legislation and Federal Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures involving the designation of federal facilities and the authorization of actions related to public trails and adoption incentives.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4500 Sunny Isle Shopping Center in Christiansted, St. Croix, United States Virgin Islands, as the 'Florence Louise Thomas Post Office.'
Document 2: This bill authorizes the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture to place on federal land at points along the American Discovery Trail acceptable signage donated to the United States for that purpose.

topic.name: Transportation Regulation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating various aspects of transportation, including safety and operational standards for rail and drone operations.

Document 1: Bakken crude oil to be transported by rail only if it has a Reid vapor pressure of not more than 9.5 pounds per square inch.
maximum volatility set by the New York Mercantile Exchange for crude oil futures contracts.
Document 2: prohibit the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) from authorizing any person to operate an unmanned aircraft system (drone) as a weapon.
manufacture, sell, or distribute a drone for such a use.
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Transportation to assess a civil penalty of $10,000 against a railroad carrier.
blockage of vehicular traffic at a grade crossing.

topic.name: Forensic Science and STEM Education
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to enhance forensic science research and promote STEM education, particularly for underrepresented groups.

Document 1: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Directs NSF to create a merit-reviewed, competitive forensic science research grants program.
Document 2: Authorize the Department of Education to provide grants to local educational agencies to encourage the ongoing development of programs and curricula for girls and underrepresented minorities in STEM.
Prepare those students to pursue undergraduate and graduate degrees and careers in such fields.
Document 3: Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to establish a nanotechnology in the schools program awarding matching grants.
Provide nanotechnology education to students and teachers.

topic.name: Retirement and Savings Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing savings and financial planning options for individuals, particularly in the context of retirement and disability-related expenses.

Document 1: establish a new retirement option for all employees
tax-exempt and contributions to, or distributions from, it are excludible from gross income
Document 2: allow tax-free rollovers of amounts in qualified tuition programs to qualified ABLE programs
designed to enable individuals with disabilities to save for and pay for disability-related expenses
Document 3: repeals provisions that limit payments from health and medical savings accounts
increase taxes on distributions from health and medical savings accounts that are not used for qualified medical expenses

topic.name: Support for Business Ownership
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting and supporting business ownership, particularly among women and private entities.

Document 1: amends the Water Resources Development Act of 2000 to authorize the Secretary of the Army to accept and expend funds contributed by a private entity
expedite the evaluation of a permit of that entity related to a project or activity for a public purpose
Document 2: establishes the Commission on Women's Business Ownership to review and make recommendations concerning the status of women business owners
the role of the federal government in aiding and promoting women business owners
Document 3: amends the Small Business Act to revise the Small Business Administration's criteria for awarding a grant to a women's business center
ability of the applicant to provide training and services to a representative number of unmarried women who are both socially and economically disadvantaged

topic.name: Native American Education and Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses the eligibility for federal services and benefits for Native Americans, as well as initiatives aimed at promoting Native American language education within tribal communities.

Document 1: Makes any U.S. citizen of Pascua Yaqui blood who is enrolled by the Pascua Yaqui Tribe eligible for the federal services and benefits made available to members of federally recognized tribes.
Document 2: Authorizes the Coquille Indian Tribe, the Confederated Tribes of the Siletz Indians, the Confederated Tribes of the Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians, the Klamath Tribes, and the Burns Paiute Tribe to obtain 99-year lease authority for trust land.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to tribes, tribal colleges or universities, tribal education agencies, schools, and private or tribal nonprofit organizations to develop and maintain, or improve and expand, programs that support the use by schools, from prekindergarten through postsecondary education, of Native American languages as their primary language of instruction.

topic.name: Legislation for Aging and Military Pay
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to financial adjustments and benefits for seniors and military personnel.

Document 1: Consumer Price Index for Seniors (CPI-S) indicates monthly changes in expenditures for consumption for individuals 62 years or older.
Document 2: Amends Social Security Act regarding totalization agreements with other countries.
Sets forth procedures for congressional consideration of such agreements.
Document 3: Waives military pay adjustment tied to Employment Cost Index and increases basic pay by 3.9%.

topic.name: Regulatory Reform and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving regulatory processes, reducing burdens on entities, and ensuring accountability within federal agencies.

Document 1: Department of Labor as a 'covered agency' for regulatory flexibility analysis provisions.
Document 2: require each agency publish in the Federal Register a biennial agricultural regulatory flexibility agenda
authorizes an agricultural entity that is adversely affected by a final agency action to seek judicial review.
Document 3: establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis in the executive branch as an independent establishment
requires Congress to pass an annual bill establishing a National Regulatory Budget that sets an overall regulatory cost cap.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: Documents discuss U.S. diplomatic efforts and sanctions related to Iran and other nations identified as state sponsors of terrorism.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State, in conjunction with the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of Defense, and the intelligence community, to conduct annual assessments of the effectiveness of sanctions against Iran.
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint a high-level U.S. representative or special envoy who shall: (1) seek to ease tensions and normalize relations between the United States and Iran.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and resources to vulnerable groups, including homeless individuals and veterans.

Document 1: expands access to Department of Defense homeowners assistance for qualified members of the Armed Forces
consult with the Secretaries of Housing and Urban Development regarding the condition of housing markets
Document 2: allows homeless veterans' comprehensive services grant funds to be used to provide funding for service center staff
direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to annually increase the rate of such payment
Document 3: deem a student independent for financial aid purposes if such student has been verified as both a homeless child or youth
requires such a verification to take place during the school year in which the application for such aid is submitted

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving public health and safety through awareness campaigns, funding requirements, and research initiatives.

Document 1: Prohibits a U.S. government grant, contract, or cooperative agreement for the reconstruction of Iraq to be awarded unless the government of Iraq provides at least 50% matching funds from non-U.S. government contributions.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop a national Shaken Baby Syndrome public health campaign.
Directs the Secretary to carry out communication, education, and training about Shaken Baby Syndrome prevention.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to expand and intensify research to develop advanced imaging technologies for prostate cancer detection.
Directs the Secretary to carry out a national campaign to increase awareness and knowledge with respect to the need for prostate cancer screening.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections in financial transactions and credit reporting.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act to restrict the award of court costs and attorney fees to the defendant in an action to enforce liability for violating the Act solely upon a finding that the action was brought in bad faith and for the purpose of harassment.
Clarification of practices surrounding debt collection.
Document 2: Declares that failure by a financial institution or its representatives to report a suspicious transaction is not a violation if certain conditions are met, emphasizing good faith efforts.
Establishes guidelines for reporting suspicious transactions in financial institutions.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to require disclosure of credit scores to consumers as part of their annual credit report.
Requires consumer reporting agencies to provide consumer information for financial education.

topic.name: Spectrum Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving the efficiency and management of electromagnetic spectrum use by federal entities and emergency responders.

Document 1: requires the National Telecommunications and Information Administration to conduct prize competitions to accelerate the development and commercialization of technology that improves spectrum efficiency
The Federal Communications Commission must publish a technical paper providing criteria that may be used for the design of such competitions.
Document 2: amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to make amounts available from the Spectrum Relocation Fund
the bill also provides for incumbent federal entities to receive payment from the SRF for relocation and sharing costs incurred to accommodate sharing spectrum frequencies
Document 3: establish the First Responders Interoperable Device Availability Trust Fund

requires that amounts from the Fund be used by the Administrator of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration to make grants, beginning in FY2012, to purchase interoperable devices for use by public safety personnel

topic.name: Military Reserve Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on the benefits and support systems for members of the military reserves and their families.

Document 1: enrollment of members of the National Guard and reserve in health and dental care programs for veterans
education and outreach about the VA programs, benefits, and services
Document 2: days of active duty or active service used to reduce the minimum age at which a member of the reserves may retire
non-regular (reserve) service
Document 3: death gratuity for a member of the Armed Forces who dies while serving
payable to such parent, brother, or sister as designated by the member

topic.name: Lead Exposure Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at reducing lead exposure in residential and educational environments.

Document 1: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992
set the environmental intervention blood lead level for a confirmed concentration of lead
Document 2: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act by requiring the Environmental Protection Agency to publish revised guidance
testing protocols for schools to detect lead contamination in school drinking water

topic.name: Labor Rights and Education
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing labor rights, education, and training opportunities within various sectors, particularly in healthcare and higher education.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Labor to establish a partnership grant program to award matching grants to eligible entities to carry out comprehensive programs to provide education to nurses
create a pipeline to nursing for incumbent ancillary health care workers who wish to advance their careers.
Document 2: Directs the head of any federal agency that awards or obligates funds for any construction contract to ensure that bid specifications, project agreements, or other controlling documents do not require or prohibit a bidder from entering into, or adhering to, agreements with a labor organization.
otherwise discriminate against such a party because it did or did not become a signatory or otherwise adhere to such an agreement.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide collective bargaining rights for students enrolled at a private institution of higher education who perform work for remuneration
regardless of whether the work relates to their courses of study.

topic.name: Immigration and Legal Protections
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights and protections of individuals in immigration detention and related legal processes.

Document 1: Humane Enforcement and Legal Protections for Separated Children Act
apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities
Document 2: American Anti-Torture Act of 2009
prohibit any person in custody from inhumane treatment
Document 3: Habeas Corpus Restoration Act of 2007
restoring habeas corpus rights for detained individuals

topic.name: Early Childhood Education Funding
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing early childhood education and support for at-risk students through funding and mentoring programs.

Document 1: Requires states to issue guidance to local educational agencies regarding permissible uses of school improvement funds to serve preschool children.
Directs the Secretary of Education to issue guidance to LEAs on the use of funds for prekindergarten programs.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to improve the capacity of principals to increase student achievement.
Establish learning communities where principals and teachers work together to improve teaching and learning.
Document 3: Establishes a transition-to-success mentoring program to assist at-risk students in transitioning from middle to high school.
Requires grantees to assign success coaches to at-risk students to create and implement success plans.

topic.name: Voting and Administrative Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislation related to voting regulations and the provision of administrative services in the context of federal operations.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies operating overseas to participate in a specific administrative support system.
Requires detailed explanations for not participating in the system.
Document 2: Amends the National Voter Registration Act to include statements regarding the deportability of aliens.
Requires notices at polling places about the violation of federal law for noncitizens voting.
Document 3: Directs the Election Assistance Commission to reimburse states for online voter registration program costs.
Establishes requirements for online voter registration programs.

topic.name: Animal Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting animal rights and preventing cruelty through regulatory measures.

Document 1: prohibit the use or possession of body-gripping traps
civil fine of up to $500 for each body-gripping trap possessed or used
Document 2: make it unlawful for a licensed firearms dealer to authorize an employee to possess or transfer a firearm
civil and criminal penalties for knowingly violating the requirements
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term for possessing, shipping, transporting, purchasing, selling, delivering, or receiving any horse
intent that it be used for human consumption

topic.name: Aviation Security Regulations
topic.description: Documents focus on regulations and measures to enhance security in commercial aviation, particularly regarding access to flight decks and covert testing of security systems.

Document 1: installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck
barriers remain locked while the aircraft is in flight
Document 2: installation of secondary barriers, other than the cockpit door
barriers on an aircraft with a cockpit door to remain locked while the aircraft is in flight
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to ensure no advance notice of a covert test is provided
study the impact of implementing covert testing procedures on transportation security

topic.name: Food Safety and Labeling Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on food safety, labeling, and nutritional assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Food Assistance Act of 1983 to provide for the increased purchase of Kosher and Halal food
modify the labeling of the commodities list under the emergency food assistance program
Document 2: establish the local food for healthy families program
provide low-income families... with incentives to purchase fruits and vegetables
Document 3: requires the market name of genetically modified salmon to include 'Genetically Engineered'
conducts its own environmental assessment of the salmon

topic.name: Alaska Native Community Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at recognizing and supporting the rights and resources of Alaska Native communities.

Document 1: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation pursuant to this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation pursuant to this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Native American Programs Act of 1974 to reauthorize appropriations
to promote the economic and social self-sufficiency of Native Americans.

topic.name: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at enhancing the rights and protections of servicemembers under the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act.

Document 1: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to provide that if a court renders a temporary order for custodial responsibility for a child based solely on a deployment or anticipated deployment of a parent-servicemember
Prohibits a servicemember's deployment or possible deployment from being considered as the sole factor in the determination of a child's best interest
Document 2: This bill amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) to make any predispute arbitration agreement invalid or unenforceable against a servicemember
A servicemember's waiver of any SCRA rights and protections shall be effective only if made after a specific dispute has arisen

topic.name: Postal Service and Customer Service Standards
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the operational standards and customer service delivery of the United States Postal Service and federal agencies.

Document 1: Repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) on January 5, 2015
directs USPS to reinstate the service standards that were in effect on December 31, 2011.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to develop performance measures to determine whether federal agencies are providing high-quality customer service
develop standards to be met by agencies to provide high-quality customer service and improve service delivery.
Document 3: Makes procedures governing the closure or consolidation of postal branches, stations, or other facilities the same as those applicable to post offices.

topic.name: Law Enforcement and Cybercrime Prevention
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing law enforcement capabilities and preventing cybercrime, particularly in rural areas and among vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize appropriations for rural drug enforcement assistance.
Training rural law enforcement officers in the investigation of drug trafficking and related crimes.
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to make two-year grants for Internet crime awareness and cybercrime prevention programs.
Provide professional training to teachers and school personnel on cybercrime awareness and prevention.
Document 3: Makes emergency appropriations for state and local law enforcement assistance under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant program.

topic.name: Federal Funding Restrictions on Family Planning
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting federal funding for population control and family planning activities, including abortion procedures.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.

topic.name: National Nurse for Public Health
topic.description: Legislation establishing the role of a National Nurse for Public Health to enhance public health leadership, nursing coordination, and community health education.

Document 1: establish the position of National Nurse for Public Health within the Office of the Surgeon General
providing leadership and coordination of Public Health Service nursing professional affairs
Document 2: require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to designate the Chief Nurse Officer as the National Nurse for Public Health

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Usage
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, transfer, and usage of federal lands and resources, including regulations on commercial activities and agreements within national parks.

Document 1: Transfers certain land in Riverside and San Diego counties, California, from the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the United States to be held in trust for the Pechanga Band of Luiseno Mission Indians.
Prohibits such transfer from including the 12.82 acres of lands more or less, including the facilities, improvements, and appurtenances associated with the existing 230kV transmission line.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to require a permit and assess an annual fee of $200 for commercial filming activities on federal land.
Bars the Secretary from prohibiting the use of cameras or related equipment used for commercial filming activities on federal land.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer specified funds previously appropriated to the National Park Service to counties within the Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
